seoTitle: How to Use Canvases in Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Create and format collaborative Canvas documents in Slack, add checklists and callouts, and pin a canvas to a project channel.
intro: Keeping team wikis and action items accessible shouldn't mean jumping to an external document tool. This guide covers creating a Slack Canvas, formatting it with headers and checklists, and pinning it to a channel for easy access.

steps.heading: Open the Canvases panel
steps.body: Click the Files tab in the left sidebar, then select Canvases to open your document management view.

steps.heading: Create a new canvas
steps.body: Click 'New Canvas' in the upper right to launch a blank document.

steps.heading: Format your content
steps.body: Type a title and use the formatting ribbon to add headers, checklists, and colored callout boxes.

steps.heading: Add a cover image
steps.body: Open the canvas menu, select 'Add Cover Image', and upload a brand header for a more polished look.

steps.heading: Link the canvas to a channel
steps.body: Navigate to a project channel, click the canvas header icon, and link the document directly to that channel.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit a canvas at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, canvases support real-time collaborative editing similar to a shared document.

faq.question: How do I control who can edit a canvas?
faq.answer: Use the sharing permissions toggle to grant workspace members read or edit access.

faq.question: Can I comment on specific parts of a canvas?
faq.answer: Yes, highlighting text lets you drop a comment that opens a contextual discussion thread.

faq.question: Is there a template gallery for canvases?
faq.answer: Yes, you can start from pre-built templates instead of a blank document if your workspace has them available.
